# Axion Mobile 1.0.3

**Bug fix: scrubbing actually scrubs now.**

A small follow-up to v1.0.2. Drops in over any prior 1.x build; library
and settings are preserved.

---

## What's fixed

### Seeking on the now-playing scrubber

In v1.0.2 (and earlier), dragging the progress bar to a different point
in the song looked like it worked for a frame, then snapped right back
and the audio kept playing from where it was. This was two bugs stacked:

1. **Stale gesture closure.** The pan gesture's `onEnd` callback runs as
   a Reanimated worklet. Worklets capture JS values at construction
   time, so the "final position" computed inside `onEnd` was actually
   the position from when your finger first touched down — i.e. the
   current playback time. The seek call was firing, but with the wrong
   number, so the song jumped to where it already was.
2. **Display lag on release.** Even after the seek call landed,
   `react-native-track-player`'s `useProgress` polls every 250 ms.
   Clearing the "scrubbing" flag on touch-release showed the live
   position before the new one had been reported, producing a visible
   snap-back to the old time.

v1.0.3 reads the release point straight from the gesture event and
hands it to a JS-thread function that has live access to the bar width
and track duration, so the seek target is always correct. It also
keeps the optimistic position visible on the bar until `useProgress`
catches up (within ~0.75 s, or after a 1.5 s safety timeout).

Net result: drag, release, and the song actually plays from the spot
you released at.

## Downloads

| File | Size | Recommended for |
|---|---|---|
| `Axion-1.0.3-arm64-v8a.apk` | ~31 MB | **Most users.** All modern 64-bit Android phones. |
| `Axion-1.0.3-armeabi-v7a.apk` | ~25 MB | Older / cheaper 32-bit devices. |
| `Axion-1.0.3-x86_64.apk` | ~33 MB | Emulators and Chromebooks. |
| `Axion-1.0.3-universal.apk` | ~83 MB | "I don't know which one" — works on every architecture. |

## Installation

Open the new APK on top of any prior 1.x build. Same signing
certificate as 1.0.0 / 1.0.1 / 1.0.2, so Android will treat it as an
update. Library and settings are preserved.

## Verifying the APK

```powershell
Get-FileHash .\Axion-1.0.3-arm64-v8a.apk -Algorithm SHA256
```

## Full changelog

- **fix(player):** scrubbing the progress bar now actually seeks to the
  released position instead of snapping back. Pan gesture's `onEnd`
  reads the release coordinates from the event itself and computes the
  target on the JS thread, avoiding the stale worklet-captured state.
- **chore(player):** the optimistic position stays visible after
  release until `useProgress` reports a position close to the seek
  target, eliminating the half-second visual snap.
- **chore(version):** bump to 1.0.3 (`versionCode` 10003).

(No changes to imports / library / playback engine since 1.0.2.)

## Reporting issues

Open an issue with:

- Axion Mobile version (Settings → About)
- Phone model + Android version
- Steps to reproduce
- Logs via `adb logcat -s AxionMobile:V ReactNativeJS:V *:S` if you can capture them
